name: Chinese Steamed Buns
recipeIngredient: 3 cups all-purpose flour (reserve 1 tablespoon for mixing by hand)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, sprinkle yeast and sugar evenly over the lukewarm water; stir until yeast dissolves. Let stand 10 minutes or until foamy.
2. In a large bowl or food processor, combine flour and yeast mixture and mix well, or process 1 minute. If mixing by hand, sprinkle reserved 1 tablespoon flour on work surface and turn dough out onto work surface. Knead until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
3. Spray a large bowl with nonstick cooking spray. Place dough in bowl. Cover loosely with plastic wrap or damp towel. Let rise until dough triples in volume, about 3 hours. Punch down and wrap in plastic until ready to use. Keeps up to 2 months in the freezer.
Mushroom Marinade:
1/4 tablespoon hoisin sauce
1/4 tablespoon chili sauce
1/4 tablespoon rice wine or dry sherry (omit if desired)
1/4 tablespoon low-sodium soy sauce
1/4 teaspoon minced scallion whites
1/4 clove minced garlic
1/2-inch piece pared fresh ginger root, minced
6 oz portabella mushroom caps (or 1 pound, 4 oz pork loin)
Steamed Buns with Dipping Sauce:
1/4 cup finely diced medium red bell pepper
2 tablespoons finely chopped scallions (both white and green parts)
1 minced garlic clove
Marinated mushrooms from above recipe
1/4 cup whole water chestnuts, finely diced
1 tablespoon hoisin sauce
1 tablespoon all-purpose flour
Bun dough (see above recipe)
3 tablespoons low-sodium soy sauce
1 teaspoon white vinegar
1/4 teaspoon hot chili oil
Chopped cilantro for garnish
1. Cook bell pepper, scallions, and garlic in a large non-stick pan until peppers are soft, 5-8 minutes.
2. Add mushrooms with marinade to the pan; cook until most liquid has evaporated, about 3-4 minutes. Cover and refrigerate until ready to use.
3. Sprinkle work surface with flour. Turn bun dough out onto prepared surface and knead 1 minute. Roll into a log, about 16 inches long and 1 1/2 inches wide.
4. Cut dough into 16 equal pieces and roll each into a ball. Flatten each ball with the palm of your hand into a 3-inch diameter circle. Place a heaping teaspoon of filling mixture in center of each dough round. Gather edges to enclose filling, twisting together and pressing to seal. Repeat with remaining dough and filling. Cover buns with plastic wrap.
5. Line steamer rack with cheesecloth or wax paper (use cabbage leaves if available). Place in large shallow saucepan or wok; add 2 inches of water. Bring to rolling boil. Arrange 8 buns at least 1 inch apart on prepared steamer rack. Cover and steam until puffy, tender, and cooked through, about 15-20 minutes. Repeat with remaining buns, adding water as necessary.
6. Prepare dipping sauce: In a small bowl, combine soy sauce, 1 tablespoon water, reserved scallions, vinegar, reserved garlic, and hot chili oil (omit if desired). Sprinkle with cilantro if using.
Serve freshly steamed buns immediately with dipping sauce on the side, or cool and freeze for later use. To reheat, thaw until soft and steam until warmed through, about 5 minutes. Enjoy! The recipe also suggests using other vegetables and sauces to make different fillings.
